symptom: Frontend received HTML for an API route and showed a JSON parse error.
expected: GET /api/browser/capabilities through port 5173 returns JSON proxied from the backend.
actual: Port 5173 returned the Vite HTML shell before the dev server restart.
evidence: Response content type was text/html and body started with <!doctype html>.
root_cause_hypothesis: Vite dev server was stale or proxy config was not loaded.
confirmed_root_cause: Restarting the dev server from the active UI folder made the same route return application/json.
attempted_fix: Restarted the local dev server from the folder containing vite.config.js.
do_not_repeat: After route/proxy changes, restart the dev server and test through both 4177 and 5173 before calling the UI fixed.
next_safe_action: Keep proxy test in the Browser validation checklist.

symptom: Raw Unexpected token '<' error appeared when the frontend parsed Vite HTML as JSON.
expected: Frontend reports a readable non-JSON API error instead of crashing on response.json().
actual: The API helper called response.json() without checking content type.
evidence: src/main.jsx api helper parsed every response as JSON.
root_cause_hypothesis: Frontend blind JSON parsing hides the real route/proxy failure.
confirmed_root_cause: The helper did not check content type before parsing.
attempted_fix: Added content-type validation and readable non-JSON error messages.
test_run: Pending after code change and reload.
do_not_repeat: Never parse API responses as JSON before checking content-type and preserving a short response excerpt.
next_safe_action: Restart backend if needed, reload UI, and force a mock/provider test plus endpoint checks.

symptom: Full ChatGPT controlled-browser round trip is not proven and may be blocked by login or human verification.
expected: Prompt is pasted into ChatGPT, submitted, response is detected, and final answer box is filled.
actual: Playwright and Chromium are installed, but ChatGPT controlled-browser automation remains blocked or unproven.
evidence: Browser panel shows a controlled-browser warning for ChatGPT and prior attempts hit human-check or auth friction.
root_cause_hypothesis: ChatGPT may reject controlled browsers or require manual login/verification.
confirmed_root_cause: Not confirmed in this loop; blocker risk remains active.
attempted_fix: Do not bypass. Add stage logging and mock/manual fallback so surrounding app plumbing can be tested without repeating blocked automation attempts.
do_not_repeat: Do not keep retrying ChatGPT controlled-browser automation when verification or login blockers are visible; log the blocker and switch to mock/manual fallback work.
next_safe_action: Validate deterministic mock provider route and manual fallback.

symptom: Malformed JSON sent to an API route returned the Express HTML error page.
expected: Malformed JSON and unknown /api routes return JSON errors through both backend and Vite proxy.
actual: Before this loop, body-parser errors could return text/html with a stack page.
evidence: Direct bad-json test against POST /api/browser/consult previously returned a 400 text/html body starting with an HTML document.
root_cause_hypothesis: Express JSON parser errors and unknown API routes were not converted into the app's JSON error envelope.
confirmed_root_cause: No JSON parse error middleware or API 404 handler existed before static/fallback handling.
attempted_fix: Added JSON parse error middleware, an /api 404 JSON handler, and an API error handler.
test_run: curl bad JSON and unknown /api route through ports 4177 and 5173.
test_result: 400 application/json {"ok":false,"error":"Invalid JSON body."}; 404 application/json {"ok":false,"error":"API route not found: GET /api/browser/not-a-real-route"}.
do_not_repeat: Do not leave backend API exceptions to Express default HTML handlers; test error paths through both 4177 and 5173.
next_safe_action: Keep frontend non-JSON validation and API JSON error tests in the Browser checklist.

symptom: Browser panel needed a deterministic proof that typing a draft, running consultation, and filling the final answer box works without ChatGPT automation.
expected: Test/mock provider returns a deterministic answer, shows stages, fills the final answer box, and does not auto-save.
actual: Mock provider route and frontend run-log path now work in the Browser panel.
evidence: In-app Browser panel selected Test/mock provider, entered a local draft, clicked Run automatic consultation, displayed four ok run-log stages, filled the final answer box with Browser round trip test passed., and Consultation History did not gain a new row.
root_cause_hypothesis: A deterministic provider was needed to separate app wiring from ChatGPT login/verification blockers.
confirmed_root_cause: The app wiring could be proven independently once the mock provider and visible stage log existed.
attempted_fix: Added Test/mock provider handling, visible Browser status cards, run-log stages, and final answer fill behavior.
test_run: Endpoint tests through 4177 and 5173 plus in-app Browser UI run.
test_result: 200 application/json from both ports; final answer box contained Browser round trip test passed.; save choices appeared; no automatic save occurred.
do_not_repeat: Do not call ChatGPT automation fixed from this mock result; it proves Browser panel plumbing only, not ChatGPT controlled-browser success.
next_safe_action: Keep ChatGPT automation gated and improve/manual-test the real ChatGPT path only when a logged-in visible session is ready and not blocked.

symptom: Browser mock provider and JSON error handling needed a local checkpoint before further changes.
expected: Safety-reviewed work is committed locally without push, merge, install, canonical source-file changes, private memory files, binaries, or public/private sync payloads.
actual: Local checkpoint commit was created on main and not pushed.
evidence: Commit 56c552b9fc22d608164f7f505bdcefdca31f84c3 contains server/index.js, src/main.jsx, src/styles.css, and the two Browser automation log files.
route_or_command: git commit -m "Checkpoint Browser mock provider and error logging"
root_cause_hypothesis: A checkpoint reduces risk before continuing with ChatGPT blocker reporting and manual fallback work.
confirmed_root_cause: Safety review found no forbidden files or private payloads in the changed content; generic terms like browser profile and status wording was not actual profile/private records.
attempted_fix: Committed the safe checkpoint locally only.
test_run: git status --short --branch; git diff --stat; git diff --check; full diff inspection; risk-term scan.
test_result: Clean safety review; branch main is ahead of origin/main by 1 local commit; nothing pushed.
do_not_repeat: Do not push or continue broader changes without another safety review after each loop.
next_safe_action: Improve real ChatGPT blocker reporting and manual fallback without bypassing login or verification.

symptom: Real ChatGPT automation failures needed clearer stage reporting and manual fallback controls needed to be explicit.
expected: The app shows where the real ChatGPT route got to, returns JSON for failures, stops on login/verification/unsupported-session blockers, and gives a reliable manual copy/paste fallback.
actual: Backend stages now cover browser open, ChatGPT open, page read, prompt box, prompt paste, submit click, response wait, timeout, and blocker categories. UI shows explicit fallback text, copy generated prompt, paste response into answer box, and save choices only after an answer exists.
evidence: Mock UI test still filled the final answer box and showed four ok stages; manual fallback copied the generated prompt, pasted manual fallback QA response into the final answer box, showed save choices, and did not create a new history row.
route_or_command: UI Browser panel Test/mock provider; Copy generated prompt; Paste response into answer box; GET /api/browser/capabilities; POST /api/browser/consult
root_cause_hypothesis: The useful next step was not another blind Playwright retry; it was better observability and fallback handling around known ChatGPT blockers.
confirmed_root_cause: Real ChatGPT automation remains unproven and may be blocked by login or human verification; mock/API success must not be treated as full automation success.
attempted_fix: Added richer backend stage reporting and clearer manual fallback controls without touching cookies, login, verification, or source-of-truth files.
test_run: npm run check; node --check server/index.js; endpoint tests through 4177 and 5173; in-app Browser mock provider test; in-app manual fallback copy/paste test; browser console error check.
test_result: Build passed; JSON endpoints passed; mock provider still passed; manual fallback pasted response into final answer box and showed save choices; browser console errors were empty.
do_not_repeat: Do not retry real ChatGPT automation blindly or claim it works from mock/API/manual fallback success.
next_safe_action: Only test real ChatGPT automation when the visible session is ready; if it blocks, log the exact stage and stop.

symptom: A real ChatGPT timeout or no-response result could be returned as ok:false but still be labeled as an answered success by the route/UI.
expected: If ChatGPT automation times out or fails before capturing a response, the backend returns JSON with status failed and the frontend shows failed/manual fallback rather than answered/opened.
actual: Patched the route to return status failed for ok:false non-blocked results and patched the UI to label those results Failed and activate manual fallback.
evidence: Code inspection found the route only special-cased result.blocked before the answered success branch. Regression tests after the patch kept mock provider and manual fallback working.
route_or_command: POST /api/browser/consult; Browser panel Test/mock provider; Paste response into answer box
root_cause_hypothesis: The real route needed an explicit ok:false branch for timeouts/no-response failures that are not login/verification blockers.
confirmed_root_cause: The previous route flow would fall through to the answered response branch for ok:false non-blocked results.
attempted_fix: Added an ok:false result branch in /api/browser/consult and frontend failed-result handling for manual fallback activation.
test_run: node --check server/index.js; npm run check; restarted dev server; GET /api/browser/capabilities through 5173; POST mock consult through 5173; bad JSON through 5173; unknown /api through 5173; Browser UI mock provider; Browser UI manual fallback.
test_result: All regression tests passed; mock final answer filled; generated prompt copied; pasted manual response filled the answer box; save choices appeared; history count stayed unchanged.
do_not_repeat: Do not allow no-response or timeout results to be labeled as answered; do not run real ChatGPT unless the controlled session is visibly ready and no login/verification bypass is needed.
next_safe_action: Ask Alex to confirm the controlled ChatGPT session is ready with Temporary Chat enabled before one real-route test.

symptom: Real ChatGPT automatic consultation stopped before prompt paste because the controlled browser reached a human-verification challenge.
expected: LifePlanSystem input opens ChatGPT, pastes the prepared harmless prompt, submits it, waits for the response, captures REAL_ROUTE_TEST_OK, and fills the final answer box.
actual: The backend reached ChatGPT but the visible page state was a Cloudflare/human-verification challenge. The prompt was not proven pasted, submit was not clicked, no response was captured, and the final answer box stayed empty.
evidence: UI run log showed ok Backend route reached, ok Preparing prompt, ok Opening browser, ok Opening ChatGPT, ok Waiting for page, then blocked Blocked by human verification. The displayed ChatGPT URL contained a Cloudflare challenge token and was redacted from logs.
route_or_command: Browser panel Run automatic consultation with target_agent=ChatGPT and no selected context files
root_cause_hypothesis: ChatGPT/Cloudflare blocks the Playwright-controlled persistent browser before the composer is available.
confirmed_root_cause: Controlled browser page showed a human-verification challenge before prompt-box detection could proceed.
attempted_fix: No bypass or retry. Stopped after the single approved real-route attempt and kept manual fallback visible.
test_run: UI Browser panel real ChatGPT run with harmless draft: LifePlanSystem Browser real-route test. Please reply exactly: REAL_ROUTE_TEST_OK
test_result: Blocked by human verification; REAL_ROUTE_TEST_OK did not reach the final answer box; save response remained disabled; manual fallback controls remained visible; API/proxy status stayed connected.
do_not_repeat: Do not retry the same controlled-browser route while the human-verification challenge is visible; do not bypass verification; do not log full challenge-token URLs; do not call real ChatGPT automation fixed.
next_safe_action: Only run another real-route test if Alex completes verification and the controlled browser reaches a normal temporary ChatGPT composer; otherwise keep improving mock/manual fallback and blocker messaging.

issue_chosen: Make manual/external fallback the primary safe Browser route after the confirmed controlled ChatGPT human-verification blocker.
expected: Browser panel defaults to a safe manual/external workflow, keeps mock provider as regression proof, clearly labels controlled ChatGPT as blocked/experimental, avoids another real ChatGPT retry, and only enables save choices after an answer reaches the final answer box.
actual: Manual / External ChatGPT is the default provider; controlled Open/Copy + Open/Run automatic are disabled for ChatGPT URLs with the confirmed blocker message; a separate manual paste box and Use pasted response button gate the final answer box; mock provider still fills the final answer box; backend returns JSON for manual/external guard, mock, malformed JSON, and unknown API routes.
evidence: UI test showed default target Manual / External ChatGPT, run/open disabled, generated prompt copied, pasted response remained in manual box until Use pasted response, final answer filled after Use pasted response, save choices appeared only after final answer existed, history count stayed unchanged, mock provider returned Browser round trip test passed, and Controlled ChatGPT showed the human-verification blocker without running automation.
commands_run: Restarted npm run dev from the active UI folder
In-app Browser UI fallback, mock, and controlled-status checks
test_result: Passed. Manual/external fallback is primary and tested; mock provider still works; JSON routes stay JSON; controlled ChatGPT is visibly blocked and was not retried.
root_cause: Controlled ChatGPT Playwright automation is blocked by ChatGPT/Cloudflare human verification; the reliable product path is manual/external fallback now and API/local model integration later.
do_not_repeat: Do not keep retrying controlled ChatGPT Playwright after Cloudflare/human verification is confirmed. Use manual/external fallback or proper API/local model paths instead.
next_safe_action: Commit the safe fallback/status/logging changes locally, do not push the new commit until Alex approves, and later add a real API/local model provider in a separate approved pass.

issue_chosen: Add a tested Browser consultant path that uses the existing OpenAI-compatible local endpoint instead of controlled ChatGPT automation.
expected: Selecting API/local endpoint runs through /api/browser/consult, uses the configured local endpoint, fills the final answer box, shows endpoint run-log stages, keeps save choices approval-gated, and does not touch ChatGPT or private context.
actual: The configured endpoint returned a local model answer through the Browser consult route. The Browser UI selected API/local endpoint, did not show Temporary Chat gating, filled the final answer box, showed endpoint selected/response captured stages, and left Consultation History unchanged until explicit save.
evidence: GET /api/models/runtime returned endpointConfigured true for local endpoint model qwen2.5:7b-instruct. POST /api/browser/consult target_agent=API/local endpoint returned status answered and mode api/local endpoint. UI test showed final answer length > 0, endpoint stages present, save choices visible only after final answer, and history count unchanged. Mock provider was retested successfully after the endpoint run.
commands_run: Restarted npm run dev from the active UI folder
In-app Browser UI API/local endpoint and mock provider checks
test_result: Passed. API/local endpoint is a working automatic Browser consultant path; mock provider still works; JSON error routes stay JSON; no real ChatGPT automation was retried.
root_cause: The app already had local endpoint runtime support, but Browser consultation did not expose it as a provider. Wiring it into /api/browser/consult provides reliable automation without fighting ChatGPT verification.
do_not_repeat: Do not route reliable automation through controlled ChatGPT while Cloudflare/human verification is confirmed. Prefer API/local endpoint for automatic runs and Manual / External ChatGPT for cloud fallback.
next_safe_action: Add a small UI link/status action to Settings for endpoint setup when not configured, then push only the checkpoint branch if Alex approves.

symptom: The tested API/local endpoint path was available, but Browser did not provide a direct setup/navigation action when endpoint configuration needed attention.
expected: Browser should make the browser-free provider obvious, let the user select API/local endpoint from its status card, and open Settings for endpoint setup without running ChatGPT automation.
actual: Browser now passes app navigation into BrowserConsult, shows Use API/local and Open Settings/Settings actions on endpoint status cards, selects API/local endpoint on click, and navigates to Settings on setup click.
evidence: In-app Browser test after reload showed Browser heading, Use API/local and Open Settings visible, API/local endpoint configured as http://127.0.0.1:11434 with qwen2.5:7b-instruct, Use API/local changed target_agent to API/local endpoint, Run automatic consultation remained disabled only because no draft was entered, and Open Settings navigated to Settings with endpoint setup text.
route_or_command: node --check server/index.js; npm run check; in-app Browser reload; Browser provider-card Use API/local; Browser provider-card Open Settings
root_cause_hypothesis: Users could miss the reliable browser-free path because status text did not include direct actions for provider selection or setup.
confirmed_root_cause: The Browser component did not receive setView, so it could not navigate to Settings, and the endpoint cards were informational only.
attempted_fix: Passed setView into BrowserConsult and added compact endpoint action buttons with matching styles.
test_run: Static checks plus in-app Browser UI clicks on Use API/local and Open Settings. Real ChatGPT route was not run.
test_result: Passed. Target provider changed to API/local endpoint, the run button disabled reason was only missing draft, Settings navigation worked, and the UI was returned to Browser.
do_not_repeat: Do not keep adding backend automation while the next issue is user setup discoverability; keep endpoint setup navigation visible in Browser.
next_safe_action: Run final safety diff review, commit the narrow UI/log update locally, and wait for explicit push approval.

symptom: The local checkpoint commit command failed before staging because it used a Bash-style && separator in PowerShell.
expected: Stage the four intended files and create a local checkpoint commit without pushing main or the branch.
actual: PowerShell rejected the combined command with 'The token && is not a valid statement separator in this version.' No files were staged or committed by that failed command.
evidence: Git command output showed a PowerShell ParserError at the && separator.
route_or_command: git add ... && git commit -m "Improve Browser local endpoint setup flow"
root_cause_hypothesis: The shell is Windows PowerShell, not Bash.
confirmed_root_cause: PowerShell parser rejected && before Git ran the commit step.
attempted_fix: Log the mistake and rerun staging and commit as separate PowerShell commands.
test_run: Failed local Git command only; no app code or endpoint was executed.
test_result: Failed command logged; no repository content was changed by the failed command beyond this log entry.
do_not_repeat: Do not use Bash-style && in this Windows PowerShell session; run Git staging and commit as separate commands.
next_safe_action: Run git add and git commit as separate commands, then verify status and latest commit.

symptom: The API/local endpoint path still used cloud-only labels, making the working local route look like the blocked ChatGPT/cloud browser route.
expected: When API/local endpoint is selected, Browser should label the run action and disabled reason as endpoint-specific while preserving manual/cloud fallback wording where appropriate.
actual: The provider label is Consultant provider, API/local shows Run API/local consultation, the disabled reason says to enter a message before running the API/local endpoint, and the paste box says Captured AI response or manual paste.
evidence: In-app Browser test after reload selected API/local and found runButtonText Run API/local consultation, runButtonTitle Enter a message before running the API/local endpoint, Consultant provider visible, Captured AI response or manual paste visible, and the old Captured cloud response or manual paste label absent.
route_or_command: node --check server/index.js; npm run check; in-app Browser reload; select API/local; inspect labels
root_cause_hypothesis: The UI copy was written when Browser was mainly framed as a cloud consultant flow.
confirmed_root_cause: Shared labels and disabled reasons did not branch for API/local endpoint.
attempted_fix: Added provider-specific run label/title copy and changed generic paste/wait text from cloud-only wording to AI/answer wording. A first patch attempt failed due stale context and was replaced with smaller hunks after re-reading exact lines.
test_run: Static checks and in-app Browser label verification. Real ChatGPT route was not run.
test_result: Passed. API/local selected state shows local endpoint wording and no old cloud-response paste label.
do_not_repeat: Do not let API/local endpoint UI inherit cloud-browser wording; re-read exact nearby lines before patching mixed JSX sections.
next_safe_action: Run safety diff review, commit the wording cleanup locally, and wait for explicit push approval.

symptom: After Build prompt, the Browser response panel could say Waiting for AI response even though only a local prompt had been built and no request had been sent or browser opened.
expected: Typing and building a prompt should show a prompt-ready state, keep the final answer empty, and keep save disabled until an actual answer is captured or moved from manual paste.
actual: Browser now distinguishes prompt-ready from waiting. Build prompt with API/local selected shows Prompt ready and a prompt-built endpoint instruction, does not show Waiting for AI response, keeps final answer empty, and keeps save disabled.
evidence: In-app Browser test selected API/local, typed a harmless draft, clicked Build prompt, saw Prompt ready, did not see Waiting for AI response, saw Prompt built. Click Run API/local consultation to send it to the configured endpoint, finalAnswerLength was 0, saveButtonDisabled was true, and the generated prompt textarea included the harmless draft.
route_or_command: node --check server/index.js; npm run check; in-app Browser reload; select API/local; fill Local draft to critique; Build prompt
root_cause_hypothesis: The UI treated any consultPrompt as equivalent to an in-flight or waiting consultation.
confirmed_root_cause: waitingForExternalResponse included consultPrompt directly, so a built local prompt could trigger waiting-state copy.
attempted_fix: Added promptReadyOnly, narrowed waitingForExternalResponse to request/open result states, added responseCaptureHeading, and set provider-specific prompt-built status text in buildConsultPrompt.
test_run: Static checks and in-app Browser prompt-ready verification. Real ChatGPT route was not run.
test_result: Passed. Prompt built state is clear, final answer stayed empty, save stayed disabled, and no ChatGPT automation was attempted.
do_not_repeat: Do not treat consultPrompt alone as an in-flight response; only show waiting states after a request/open action has actually started.
next_safe_action: Run safety diff review, commit the feedback fix locally, and wait for explicit push approval.

symptom: Clicking Copy generated prompt in the automated in-app browser did not update the notice because the Clipboard API could fail when the document was not focused.
expected: Copy attempts should either show a provider-specific success notice or a clear clipboard failure notice while leaving the generated prompt visible for manual selection.
actual: Clipboard writes are now wrapped in try/catch. API/local, mock, manual, and external copy notices no longer use cloud-agent wording incorrectly. When the in-app browser test hit a document-not-focused clipboard failure, the UI showed an explicit failure message and kept the prompt visible.
evidence: In-app Browser test selected API/local, typed a harmless draft, built the prompt, clicked Copy generated prompt, and saw Clipboard copy failed: Failed to execute 'writeText' on 'Clipboard': Document is not focused.. The generated prompt remains visible for manual selection. The notice did not contain cloud agent, Prompt ready stayed visible, and the final answer stayed empty.
route_or_command: node --check server/index.js; npm run check; in-app Browser reload; select API/local; Build prompt; Copy generated prompt
root_cause_hypothesis: navigator.clipboard.writeText can reject in the automated app browser when the document is not focused, and the previous code did not catch that failure.
confirmed_root_cause: The UI reported Clipboard API failure with Document is not focused after adding catch handling.
attempted_fix: Wrapped Temporary Chat and prompt clipboard writes in try/catch, added provider-specific copy success notices, and changed the empty pasted-response notice to AI response.
test_run: Static checks and in-app Browser copy notice verification. Real ChatGPT route was not run.
test_result: Passed for failure visibility and wording. Clipboard copy itself remained blocked in the automated test by document focus, but the prompt stayed visible for manual selection and no stale cloud-agent notice appeared.
do_not_repeat: Do not assume Clipboard API writes succeed; catch failures and show a visible message with manual-selection fallback.
next_safe_action: Run safety diff review, commit the notice/clipboard feedback fix locally, and wait for explicit push approval.

symptom: The UI reported clipboard copy failure but only said the generated prompt remained visible, leaving the user to manually select a long prompt.
expected: If clipboard copy is blocked, the app should make the manual fallback immediately actionable by selecting the generated prompt and telling the user to press Ctrl+C.
actual: Prompt copy first tries navigator.clipboard.writeText, then a selected temporary textarea fallback, then focuses and selects the visible generated prompt if both copy methods fail. Clipboard read failures also show a direct paste-manually instruction.
evidence: In-app Browser test selected API/local, typed a harmless draft, built the prompt, clicked Copy generated prompt, and still hit Document is not focused. The generated prompt textarea became the active element, selectionStart was 0, selectionEnd equaled promptLength 506, the prompt included the harmless draft, the notice said press Ctrl+C, the notice had no double punctuation, and the final answer stayed empty.
route_or_command: node --check server/index.js; npm run check; in-app Browser reload; select API/local; Build prompt; Copy generated prompt
root_cause_hypothesis: The embedded browser can reject clipboard writes when the document is not focused, and a passive visible-prompt fallback is not ergonomic enough.
confirmed_root_cause: Direct clipboard write and temporary textarea copy did not copy in the automated focus state, but selecting the visible generated prompt worked.
attempted_fix: Added writeClipboardText and readClipboardText helpers, added generated prompt ref selection on copy failure, and formatted clipboard errors to remove trailing punctuation before appending UI guidance.
test_run: Static checks and in-app Browser clipboard fallback selection verification. Real ChatGPT route was not run.
test_result: Passed as fallback. Clipboard write remained blocked by focus, but the generated prompt was fully selected for Ctrl+C and the UI gave a clean instruction.
do_not_repeat: Do not stop at a passive copy failure message; select the generated prompt and give an exact Ctrl+C fallback.
next_safe_action: Run safety diff review, commit the clipboard fallback locally, and wait for explicit push approval.

symptom: Two curl POST checks to /api/browser/consult returned Invalid JSON body while checking whether Browser works in the running repo.
expected: A valid Test/mock provider request should return Browser round trip test passed through the 5173 Vite proxy.
actual: The curl commands sent malformed JSON from the Windows PowerShell command line; the app correctly rejected them with HTTP 400. Retesting with Invoke-RestMethod and ConvertTo-Json returned ok true, mode test/mock provider, status answered, and answer Browser round trip test passed.
evidence: curl.exe POST to http://127.0.0.1:5173/api/browser/consult returned {"ok":false,"error":"Invalid JSON body."}; Invoke-RestMethod with a ConvertTo-Json body returned stages Backend route reached, Preparing prompt, Provider running, and Response captured.
route_or_command: curl.exe -X POST http://127.0.0.1:5173/api/browser/consult ...; Invoke-RestMethod -Uri http://127.0.0.1:5173/api/browser/consult -Method Post -ContentType application/json -Body ($body | ConvertTo-Json -Compress)
root_cause_hypothesis: The failure was command quoting, not the Browser consult route.
confirmed_root_cause: The same route passed once the JSON body was generated by PowerShell ConvertTo-Json.
attempted_fix: Retested with Invoke-RestMethod and ConvertTo-Json, then logged the failed curl method.
test_run: GET /api/browser/capabilities through 4177 and 5173; POST /api/browser/consult through 5173 with Test/mock provider.
test_result: Capabilities returned JSON on both ports; Test/mock provider returned Browser round trip test passed through 5173.
do_not_repeat: Do not hand-escape JSON bodies for curl from Windows PowerShell when testing Browser routes; use ConvertTo-Json or a file-backed request body.
next_safe_action: Report that Browser wiring works in the running LifePlanSystemPublic checkpoint checkout, while controlled ChatGPT automation remains unproven and should not be retried unless the visible session is ready.

symptom: The first in-app Browser UI test could not select the Consultant provider because getByLabel('Consultant provider') found zero controls.
expected: The UI provider selector should be selectable so Test/mock provider can prove the Browser panel round trip from the visible app.
actual: The visible label text was not programmatically associated with the select, so the label locator failed. A fresh DOM snapshot showed two select controls; the first was the provider select with Manual / External ChatGPT, Test/mock provider, and API/local endpoint options. Selecting that control, filling the draft, and clicking Run mock consultation filled the final answer box with Browser round trip test passed.
evidence: Browser snapshot showed text Consultant provider followed by options rather than an accessible labelled control. The recovered UI test selected Test/mock provider, filled Local draft to critique, clicked Run mock consultation, and the final answer textarea value became Browser round trip test passed. The page did not show Unexpected token or <!doctype errors.
route_or_command: in-app Browser: getByLabel('Consultant provider') failed; domSnapshot; locator('select').nth(0).selectOption('Test/mock provider'); fill Local draft; click Run mock consultation
root_cause_hypothesis: The provider select has visible label text but no label association exposed to Playwright accessibility lookup.
confirmed_root_cause: getByLabel returned zero while the DOM snapshot showed the provider select immediately after visible label text.
attempted_fix: Stopped using the failed label locator, took a fresh DOM snapshot, confirmed select count and option order, then targeted the first select as the provider control.
test_run: Visible Browser panel mock provider test in the running app at http://127.0.0.1:5173/?localendpoint=1783008027836.
test_result: Passed. Browser panel was connected, capability detection was visible, Test/mock provider filled the final answer with Browser round trip test passed, and save/sync remained gated behind user-visible buttons.
do_not_repeat: Do not assume visible label text is an accessible label in this UI; if getByLabel finds zero, take a fresh DOM snapshot and use the actual exposed control structure.
next_safe_action: Report that the Browser panel works for safe mock/API wiring in the running LifePlanSystemPublic checkpoint checkout; keep controlled ChatGPT automation marked blocked/unproven.
